phaseB.method: generalized eigenproblem H v=lam M v (M=h^3 I); fixed-asymptotic perturbations; QR-orthonormal analytic-symmetry deflation; matrix-free FD-HVP; multi-start LOBPCG-lite; battery = eps-scan + eigenvector save + overlap-with-gradient + Q_H-effect + direct quadratic-energy check.

re_relaxation.tool: production arrested-Newton (drive_production.py restart, unconstrained) -- HOLDS+CONVERGED (|Q|=0.9917 held, virial 0.998, no collapse)
re_relaxation.status: gradnorm reached ~0.12 after 400 unconstrained arrested-Newton steps -- NOT PROVEN IRREDUCIBLE. A multi-hour mode-following Riemannian-CG relaxation (fixed boundary, topology monitoring, final rescale-off) is IN PROGRESS to test whether it lowers further.

hessian_at_noncritical_field.note: res/|lam|=0.17 is NOT a tight convergence -- the eigenpair is APPROXIMATELY the lowest mode, not firmly converged; do not call it converged.
hessian_at_noncritical_field.Q_H_effect: perturbing along the mode does NOT change Q_H (0.9917 stable)
hessian_at_noncritical_field.direct_quadratic_energy: E(n+t v)-E(n) tracks 1/2 lam_euclid t^2 to ~10% (a real negative-curvature direction of the discrete second variation)
hessian_at_noncritical_field.eps_scan: lam stable to ~1e-4 across FD step 1e-3..1e-5

phaseB.verdict: UNRESOLVED. A localized negative Hessian direction exists at the field, but the field is NOT a true critical point (gradnorm ~0.12), the eigenpair is not tightly converged (res/|lam|~0.17), and the residual floor is NOT proven irreducible. A Hessian certifies (in)stability only at a critical point. The mode is orthogonal to the residual gradient, Q-preserving, and real-curvature -- most likely a grid-scale/non-criticality artifact (the Q_H=1 FS hopfion is a KNOWN stable soliton) but NOT proven either way.
phaseB.in_progress: multi-hour 256^3 mode-following relaxation (long_relax_256.py) to drive gradnorm toward a true critical point; escalate to 384^3 only after.
phaseB.gate: UNRESOLVED -- NOT PASS, NOT FAIL_H3_INSTABILITY. (Infra: a GPU-zombie holding 30.8GB caused earlier repeated 256^3 OOMs; cleared.)
